methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI training focused on worker agency in Southwest Virginia signals potential for distributed deep capability if workers learn to lead rather than simply consume AI tools, directly addressing the depth dimension outside metro hubs.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's institutional partnership with AI Ready RVA concentrates technical education infrastructure in the capital region, deepening Richmond's capability while the 'community impact' framing suggests limited reach beyond the metro footprint.

signals.title: Report says 35% of the jobs in Virginia may be at risk to artificial intelligence. Southside the least impacted, Northern Virginia the most. - Cardinal News
signals.signal_reading: The finding that 35% of Virginia jobs face AI displacement risk—with Southside least impacted and NoVA most—paradoxically signals concentration: regions with shallow AI adoption face less disruption but also miss economic opportunity, while NoVA's exposure reflects both threat and capability concentration.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents distributed capacity-building outside traditional tech corridors, but success depends on whether training develops deep AI fluency or shallow platform adoption.

signals.title: Google says it will break ground on Botetourt data center campus in coming months
signals.signal_reading: Google's Botetourt data center distributes AI infrastructure geographically but concentrates actual capability—local communities gain construction jobs and tax revenue while computational power flows elsewhere, exemplifying shallow distributed presence without depth.
